codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of evidence statistics section with two SVG horizontal bar charts (type distribution and per-direction distribution). All feature requirements met: both charts render correctly with Chinese labels, update dynamically on filter changes, use pure SVG via createElementNS, and show proper empty states. Charts are properly colored by judgment state for directions and by type for evidence types. Edge cases handled well (empty data, unknown types, missing cachedDirections). The subtitle hardcoded count '28' was appropriately removed. XSS-safe: all dynamic data in SVG uses textContent, not innerHTML.

issues.description: Direction distribution SVG viewBox is hardcoded to '0 0 600 300'. With 9 directions (jade data) this works fine, but if direction count grows significantly (20+), bars would become very cramped. Consider dynamically adjusting the viewBox height based on direction count (e.g., viewBox height = padding.top + padding.bottom + directionCount * barSpacing) and updating the SVG's viewBox attribute in renderDirectionDistributionChart(). Not a blocker with current data.

Judgment state colors are hardcoded as hex values (#10b981, #3b82f6, #f59e0b, #6b7280) rather than reading CSS custom properties. This is consistent with how other SVG charts in the project work (CSS vars are hard to use inside programmatic SVG), and AGENTS.md documents the hex values, so this is acceptable. Just noting for awareness in case the design system colors ever change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used hardcoded hex colors for judgment states in SVG chart JS code instead of CSS custom properties. This is consistent with how other dashboard charts handle it (SVG elements created via createElementNS can't easily reference CSS vars), but AGENTS.md's 'SVG Charts' section documents the hex values alongside CSS var names without clarifying that hex is acceptable/expected for programmatic SVG. Could add a note to AGENTS.md making this explicit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dashboard/evidence.html lines 604-609 (typeColors) and 728-733 (judgmentColors) use hardcoded hex. AGENTS.md 'SVG Charts' section lists both --color-judgment-* vars and hex values but doesn't state which to use in JS.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the dashboard-visual-worker skill procedure correctly: read context, planned charts, implemented with createElementNS, verified in browser with both data sources, checked for external libraries, and committed. skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure=true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows skill-prescribed order: read AGENTS.md + evidence.html + data files, implement SVG charts, start server on port 8765, use agent-browser for verification with both jade and streetwear data, then commit.

summary: Feature passes review. The implementation correctly adds a statistics section with two SVG horizontal bar charts (type distribution and per-direction distribution) to the evidence library page. Both charts update dynamically when filters are applied or removed, matching filtered evidence counts. Pure SVG with Chinese labels, proper empty states, and XSS-safe rendering. Two non-blocking notes: the direction chart viewBox could be dynamically sized for future scalability, and colors are hardcoded hex (consistent with project convention but worth documenting).
